prompt: Class.forName throws ClassNotFoundException at runtime. What is the bug?
choices: Class.forName requires the class to be imported before it can be found
Class.forName requires the fully-qualified class name; "ArrayList" should be "java.util.ArrayList"
Class.forName cannot load standard library classes, only user-defined ones
Class.forName returns null when the class is not found rather than throwing
explanation: Class.forName() looks up a class by its binary name, which must be fully qualified (e.g., "java.util.ArrayList"). Simple names like "ArrayList" are resolved only by the compiler via import statements; reflection has no import context. Fix: Class.forName("java.util.ArrayList").
